name: Mia Davis
recorded_behaviour: Final-year sports science student at Brighton. Trains five days a week; reads ingredient lists in Holland & Barrett. Last week she compared three protein bars in Sainsbury's before settling for the one with the cleanest label.

name: Liam Khan
recorded_behaviour: Apprentice in Manchester; on-site by 7am most days. Grabs whatever's quickest in the Tesco Express on the way — a meal deal sandwich, a Twix, sometimes a Grenade bar on a payday.

name: Jordan Roberts
recorded_behaviour: London barista doing back-to-back shifts. Lives off whatever the café gives away at end of day. Knows what a 'good' snack looks like from influencer orders but rarely buys his own.

name: Chloe Patel
recorded_behaviour: Works at a clothes shop in Cardiff city centre. Budget shops at Aldi every Saturday. Tracks the weekly food spend in a notes app; would rather buy a multipack of biscuits than one 'fancy' bar.

name: Tariq Hussein
recorded_behaviour: Year 2 mech eng student in Birmingham; trains at the campus gym four times a week. Tries to hit a daily protein target. Has a fixed monthly food budget and considers protein bars an expensive shortcut.

name: Ellie Foster
recorded_behaviour: Junior designer in Leeds creative quarter. Saves shopping for weekend trips to the indie deli near her flat. Posts food on her Instagram (1.4k followers) — usually with the brand tagged.

name: Sophie Adams
recorded_behaviour: Personal trainer at a boutique gym in Clapham. Helps clients plan macros and reads every label twice. Always carries a bar in her gym bag — currently rotates between Trek and KIND.

name: Marcus Wright
recorded_behaviour: Senior dev at a fintech in Manchester. Works from home three days, gym twice a week. Will pay a premium for a snack with clean ingredients but is sceptical of marketing claims.

name: Aisha Choudhury
recorded_behaviour: On the road four days a week visiting client sites. Grabs food at Pret or the station Co-op between meetings. Tired of meal-deal sandwiches but rarely has time to plan.

name: Ben Morgan
recorded_behaviour: Manages two restaurant kitchens in Bristol. Long, irregular hours. Eats whatever's quickest between services. Generally dismissive of trendy health products — thinks they're overpriced.

name: Olivia Bennett
recorded_behaviour: Full-time food blogger in east London; 28k followers on Instagram. Spends Saturday mornings at Borough Market. Quick to try a new brand if it photographs well and has a story.

name: Adam Williams
recorded_behaviour: PT in Cardiff with a 2-year-old. Trains six days a week; the small budget for snacks goes to whatever fits his macros. Sceptical of bars sold as 'healthy' but secretly indulgent on a Friday.

name: Priya Iyer
recorded_behaviour: NHS GP in Reading; juggles surgery hours and the school run. Stocks the kitchen with what the kids will eat. Suspicious of any product with a long ingredient list.

name: Tom Walker
recorded_behaviour: Marketing manager at a Bristol agency. Tries to model good food habits for the kids. Reads nutrition labels at the supermarket and has strong opinions about health-washing from brands he works adjacent to.

name: Sarah Connelly
recorded_behaviour: Liverpool primary teacher, two kids. Weekly Aldi shop. Looks for snacks the children will actually eat — and that don't cost £2 a bar. Buys multipacks of digestives, sometimes Soreen on offer.

name: Mark Foster
recorded_behaviour: Newcastle factory supervisor; three kids. Big weekly shop at Asda, every Sunday. Snack budget is for the lunchboxes — multipacks at 30p each — not individual bars at £1.80.

name: Hugo Sinclair
recorded_behaviour: Manages a small-plates restaurant in Brighton North Laine. Eats out four nights a week, shops at small grocers. Will judge a packaged snack as harshly as a chef would judge a dish.

name: Aisling O'Brien
recorded_behaviour: Belfast NHS admin; two children. Careful with the weekly food spend after household bills went up. Sceptical of premium snack pricing — thinks £1.80 is too much for a bar 'when the kids could split a packet of biscuits'.

name: Helen Carter
recorded_behaviour: Edinburgh head teacher; two kids at university. Joined a Pilates class last year. Started reading labels seriously after her 50th. Will pay for quality but expects substance behind a health claim.

name: Dean Robertson
recorded_behaviour: Sheffield electrician; four kids. Bulk shops at Costco and Aldi. Lunchbox snacks have to be cheap and unobjectionable. Allergic to premium pricing on packaged food.

name: Charlotte Davies
recorded_behaviour: Senior analyst in Edinburgh financial district. Reads the FT weekend, shops at Waitrose, holidays in wine regions. Open to a premium plant-based snack if it earns its price tag with clear quality cues.

name: Karl Schmidt
recorded_behaviour: Cambridge researcher in materials science. Reads claims like grant applications — looking for what's missing. Generally dismissive of consumer-facing marketing. Will engage if there's evidence.

name: David Mitchell
recorded_behaviour: Senior accountant in Leeds. Recent annual check-up flagged cholesterol; cardiologist suggested swapping in plant proteins. Now cautiously reading product labels for the first time in his life.

name: Linda Brown
recorded_behaviour: Semi-retired teacher in Cardiff. Shops at Tesco every Wednesday. Sticks to brands she's known for decades — Mr Kipling, Walkers. Open to trying new things if a friend recommends, but rarely from an ad.

name: Patrick Walsh
recorded_behaviour: Bristol project manager; takes the train to London twice a week. Picks up posh sandwiches at the M&S station shop. Quietly proud of his food knowledge — would resent being patronised.

name: Rachel Stone
recorded_behaviour: Aberdeen commercial property solicitor. Reads everything twice. Mistrusts brand claims by default. Will engage with a product if the substantive case stands up to scrutiny.

name: Margaret Whitcombe
recorded_behaviour: Retired primary teacher in York; widowed. Watches Bake Off, shops at M&S once a week. Plant-based anything is not in her usual basket but she's open to a clearly-explained product.

name: Geoffrey MacLeod
recorded_behaviour: Retired senior civil servant in Edinburgh. Brings home a precise weekly shop. Will tolerate a new product if its claims are stated plainly and without lifestyle flummery.

name: Doreen Phillips
recorded_behaviour: Retired hairdresser in Newcastle. Big breakfast, biscuits with tea, dinner at six. Suspicious of anything 'plant-based' — thinks it's a fad — but would humour a grandchild who brought one over.

name: Roger Hughes
recorded_behaviour: Retired Plymouth marine engineer; sceptical of every kind of marketing. Buys what his wife puts on the list. Plant-based bars would normally pass him by entirely — unless described with substance.
